qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Absolute path fallback in render tags bypasses configured template roots
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same LiquidJS root-cause path the advisory describes: `layout`/`render`/`include` resolution reaches `Loader.candidates()`, which appends `fs.fallback(file)`, and the default Node fallback resolves attacker-chosen absolute paths via `require.resolve()` outside the configured roots.

selected_finding.title: Absolute path fallback in render tags bypasses configured template roots

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code and root cause, and the copied PoC achieved the advisory impact. The same tag lookup path fell through to the default Node fallback and rendered an absolute-path file outside the configured template root.
evidence: The advisory says the `layout`, `render`, and `include` tags allow arbitrary file access because LiquidJS falls back to `require.resolve()` without constraining the resolved path to configured directories.

The finding report points to the same vulnerable path in `repo/src/fs/loader.ts`, `repo/src/fs/fs-impl.ts`, and the tag entrypoints in `repo/src/tags/render.ts`, `repo/src/tags/include.ts`, and `repo/src/tags/layout.ts`.

The copied PoC run log shows an absolute `{% render %}` rendered the vulnerable checkout's out-of-root `package.json` while `--root` pointed at an empty directory, exposing `"name": "liquidjs"` and `"version": "10.24.0"`.
The same run log then printed `[evohunt] cli root bypass confirmed` and `[evohunt] proof: ABSOLUTE_PATH_TEMPLATE_ROOT_BYPASS`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The adapted validation command rendered the vulnerable checkout's `package.json` through an absolute `{% render %}` even though `--root` pointed at an empty directory, exposing out-of-root file contents with `"name": "liquidjs"` and `"version": "10.24.0"` and printing the proof marker.
